label: State formation and interstate wars
scope: Curated major operations from the 1948 war through the 1973 counteroffensive period that can inspire large-scale campaign levels.

operations.name: Operation Nachshon
operations.summary: A Haganah offensive intended to reopen the road to Jerusalem during the 1948 war and break the siege pressure on the city.
historicalDynamics: road control decided survival
convoys needed protection across choke points
limited reserves had to be shifted quickly

quotes.speaker: David Ben-Gurion
quotes.quote: Jerusalem is the very heart of the State of Israel.
quotes.sourceNote: Ben-Gurion statement to the Knesset on Jerusalem.
quotes.relation: Campaign-adjacent framing for the Jerusalem corridor fighting that Operation Nachshon was meant to sustain.

candidateObjectives: hold corridor nodes long enough for relief convoys to pass
spend reserves to reopen blocked route segments
avoid morale collapse from repeated convoy losses
mechanicIdeas: escort timed convoy waves between map nodes
temporary road-control bonuses that can flip under pressure
reinforcement cards unlocked by restoring route continuity

operations.name: Operation Dani
operations.theater: Central front around Lydda, Ramle, and approaches to Jerusalem
operations.summary: An operation on the central front aimed at widening control around the road network leading toward Jerusalem and reducing pressure in the center of the country.
historicalDynamics: central plains and inland routes were tightly linked
rapid territorial shifts affected civilian movement and morale
command had to decide between momentum and consolidation

quotes.speaker: David Ben-Gurion
quotes.quote: Jewish Jerusalem is an organic and inseparable part of the State of Israel.
quotes.sourceNote: Ben-Gurion statement to the Knesset on Jerusalem.
quotes.relation: Campaign-adjacent political framing for the central-front and Jerusalem-corridor logic behind Operation Dani.

candidateObjectives: secure central hubs without overextending
protect routes into Jerusalem while urban pressure rises
trade mobility against static defense investments
mechanicIdeas: capture-and-hold sectors that buff nearby routes
civilian pressure events that reduce income if unresolved
momentum meter that rewards quick successive wins

operations.name: Operation Yoav
operations.summary: A southern offensive during the 1948 war intended to break the Egyptian hold on the northern Negev and reopen access to isolated areas.
historicalDynamics: desert routes and road junctions mattered more than dense urban control
southern isolation created pressure on logistics and morale
frontline breakthroughs changed strategic depth quickly

quotes.speaker: David Ben-Gurion
quotes.quote: If we do not take a stand in the Negev - Tel Aviv will not stand.
quotes.sourceNote: Ben-Gurion statement on the strategic importance of the Negev.
quotes.relation: Strong thematic fit for southern breakout and desert-corridor scenarios like Operation Yoav.

candidateObjectives: reconnect isolated southern sectors
control key desert junctions before reinforcements arrive
balance aggressive pushes with supply exhaustion
mechanicIdeas: long supply lines that weaken units when overstretched
desert visibility bonuses for recon purchases
junction capture mechanic that shortens enemy spawn paths

operations.name: Operation Horev
operations.theater: Southern desert front and Sinai approaches
operations.summary: A late-war operation meant to drive Egyptian forces from the Negev and improve Israel's bargaining position before armistice arrangements.
historicalDynamics: desert maneuver and encirclement mattered more than city-by-city fighting
weather, distance, and logistics amplified attrition
operational success had immediate political effects

quotes.speaker: David Ben-Gurion
quotes.quote: It is in the Negev that the creativity and pioneer vigor of Israel shall be tested.
quotes.sourceNote: Ben-Gurion statement on the Negev's strategic and national importance.
quotes.relation: Useful long-view framing for southern endurance scenarios like Operation Horev.

candidateObjectives: survive a long southern campaign without supply collapse
encircle high-value formations rather than chase every incursion
convert battlefield momentum into ceasefire advantage
mechanicIdeas: supply depots that determine reinforcement quality
weather turns that alter mobility and air support
armistice score that rewards territorial continuity

operations.name: Operation Kadesh
operations.summary: Israel's Sinai campaign during the Suez Crisis emphasized speed, airborne moves, and deep maneuver through open desert terrain.
historicalDynamics: tempo and surprise created operational openings
desert maneuver rewarded mobility over static defense
international timing pressure limited campaign length

quotes.speaker: David Ben-Gurion
quotes.quote: the glorious military operation that lasted seven days
quotes.sourceNote: Reported phrasing from Ben-Gurion's Knesset review of the Sinai Campaign.
quotes.relation: Fits the speed, reach, and confidence associated with Operation Kadesh.

candidateObjectives: seize distant objectives before diplomatic pressure ends the mission
use high-mobility units to avoid being fixed in place
keep logistics alive across a widening desert front
mechanicIdeas: turn-limited campaign clock
forward airstrip unlocks for faster deployments
mobility bonuses that decay when roads are cut

operations.name: Operation Focus
operations.summary: The opening air offensive of the Six-Day War sought to destroy opposing air power on the ground and create rapid air superiority.
historicalDynamics: timing and surprise were decisive
success dramatically changed all following fronts
the opening strike determined campaign tempo

quotes.speaker: Levi Eshkol
quotes.quote: Our forces were compelled to take the necessary military steps in order to put an end to this aggression and to protect human lives.
quotes.sourceNote: Eshkol address in Jerusalem during the Six-Day War.
quotes.relation: Directly matches the logic of a fast-opening air and military response such as Operation Focus.

candidateObjectives: gain a temporary campaign-wide advantage by winning an opening strike contest
commit scarce air power to the right targets within a short launch window
accept risk on one front to stabilize another
mechanicIdeas: pre-battle planning phase that buffs later waves
target priority system with incomplete intelligence
campaign modifier that changes spawn density after mission outcome

operations.name: Operation Abiray-Lev
operations.theater: Suez Canal crossing during the Yom Kippur War
operations.summary: A mid-war Israeli counteroffensive that crossed the Suez Canal and sought to reverse battlefield momentum after the initial surprise of the Yom Kippur War.
historicalDynamics: opening setbacks forced rapid adaptation
bridging and crossing points became strategic bottlenecks
counterattack timing mattered as much as force size

quotes.speaker: Moshe Dayan
quotes.quote: This is the end of the Third Temple.
quotes.sourceNote: Reported crisis remark from the opening phase of the Yom Kippur War.
quotes.relation: Captures the existential mood that the Suez counteroffensive and Operation Abiray-Lev were meant to reverse.

candidateObjectives: recover from a disadvantaged opening state
protect bridging assets long enough to reverse momentum
manage reserve mobilization under heavy pressure
mechanicIdeas: battlefield state that starts with debuffs and improves through objectives
bridgehead zones that unlock new lanes when held
reserve mobilization mechanic that costs economy to restore army strength
